originalBehavior: The main route opens an editable systems canvas with toolbars, sidebar controls, modal overlays, and URL-loaded model state.
localStatus: The upstream v1.1 editor remains intact and now serves as the route root, with only metadata cleanup and the shared footer layered around it.
notes: The local port preserves the editor-first workflow instead of shipping the upstream marketing landing page.
Canvas sizing, modal behavior, and edit-play mode switching remain owned by the original runtime.
evidence: Local route walkthrough on 2026-03-10 confirmed the editor opens directly and loads blank or encoded models.
Source tree still centers the runtime on Loopy.js plus the modal and page UI helpers.

originalBehavior: Users add nodes and labels, rename them inline, tune starting values, and reposition the system diagram by drawing and dragging directly on the canvas.
localStatus: Node, label, and drag authoring remain in the upstream toolchain without a wrapper rewrite.
notes: The sidebar still edits labels, colors, and starting values through the original component system.
The local cleanup touched copy only, not the creation or manipulation handlers.
evidence: Parity scope includes authoring because the route is primarily a live modeling tool.
Source files explicitly separate ink, drag, erase, label, and sidebar editing concerns.

originalBehavior: Users draw arrows between nodes, flip them positive or negative, and vary effect strength through the sidebar to change feedback-loop behavior.
localStatus: Signed-edge editing and repeated-arrow strength logic stay in the original upstream model and sidebar code.
notes: Relationship tuning still uses the upstream slider art and edge serialization model.
The local route preserves delayed relationships by keeping arrow geometry and timing logic unchanged.
evidence: The routed editor still supports positive and negative loops plus repeated arrows for stronger effects.
Source orientation notes in compilations/ncase-loopy.md call out signed link tuning as a core module.

originalBehavior: The model can switch between edit and play mode, run signals through the graph, reset state, and change simulation speed with transport controls.
localStatus: Play mode, reset behavior, and speed tuning remain in the original control bar and model update loop.
notes: Edit-to-play transitions still reset or preserve the same state transitions as upstream.
Embedded remix mode continues to use the upstream query-string-driven play controls.
evidence: Local smoke testing covers play, reset, and speed changes because they are the tool's main feedback loop.
The upstream runtime still uses the same 30 FPS update cycle and signal speed slider.

originalBehavior: Users can open sample models, load them into the editor, and immediately remix them through the same live toolchain.
localStatus: The example gallery remains modal-driven, but its cards now target local ?data=... URLs instead of the original host.
notes: The built-in sample models remain available and still open at the route root for remixing.
External user-made example listings were intentionally removed from the public surface.
evidence: The local examples modal still loads sample systems and returns control to the main editor.
The recheck plan explicitly called out example loading as a parity target for loopy.

originalBehavior: The tool serializes models into query strings for saving, remixing, and embedding, and also exports or imports plain-text model files.
localStatus: Serialization, embed generation, and file import-export remain upstream-driven, with URLs now resolving from the local route root.
notes: Saved links and embed code now point to the local GitHub Pages-safe subpath automatically.
The neutral about panel replaces the old public credit page, but save, embed, and export flows stay intact.
evidence: Smoke review targeted save-link and embed output because the tool depends on URL serialization for sharing.
The upstream code still generates ?data=... and ?embed=1&data=... links from window.location.
